kept.text:
Breaking News! 🇮🇱

Israeli singer Omer Adam is making headlines in the business world after his AI company signed a $1 billion deal with global infrastructure giant Crusoe, marking a major win for Israel’s growing tech sector. 

The agreement will bring Crusoe into Israel for the first time through the partnership.  

Adam’s company, Anan, will establish and operate a 40-megawatt AI server farm at its data center in the northern Israeli city of Afula. The site is expected to expand in the future as demand for AI computing power continues to rise.  

The company said the full project could eventually reach 100 megawatts and span tens of thousands of square meters, using advanced cooling systems, backup power, and high-performance infrastructure designed for training AI models.  

The deal is being seen as another sign of Israel’s growing role in the global AI race, with major international firms increasingly investing in Israeli technology and infrastructure.

profile_bio.description: International Speaker, Bestselling Author, CEO of SMA, and Business Consultant | Harvard & UChicago Grad | Visit My Website: https://t.co/rY0J9gZ5I1

kept.text: Neoclouds are now involved with every single hyperscaler and major AI Lab to date. Nscale, for example, bought the Monarch Compute Campus, signing a 1.35GW LoI with Microsoft for datacenter AND compute leasing. Meanwhile Crusoe is deploying 13k AMD MI355X chips. How does anyone track this? (2/4)
